digest.component_or_area: GitHub Actions release workflow supply-chain pinning
propositions: Release workflow references first-party actions by moving version tags rather than full commit SHAs, so the same source can execute different code across runs.
Release checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token persisted in local git config.
Build tools are installed with unrestricted upgrades instead of a hash-locked constraints file, and Hatchling's version is not enforced without --no-isolation against a pre-installed locked env.
The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without a digest/signature check in a job holding id-token: write.
The fix splits MCP publishing into an unprivileged mcp_verify job that verifies the archive digest and an OIDC-privileged mcp_registry job that consumes only the verified artifact.
New lint and test workflows enforce SHA pins, persist-credentials, --no-isolation, checksum verification, and the docs Pinning section.
